title: podman selinux test failure for downstream podman on RHEL 8.9 and RHEL 9.3
body:
### Issue Description

It's failed to run test/system/410-selinux.bats for downstream podman both on RHEL 8.9 and RHEL 9.3, for details, please see the following test steps.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. dnf install podman podman-tests
2. install bats
3. cd /usr/share/podman
4. export PODMAN=/usr/bin/podman
5. bats test/system/410-selinux.bats


### Describe the results you received

[root@kvm-02-guest10 podman]# bats test/system/410-selinux.bats
410-selinux.bats
 ✓ podman selinux: confined container
 ✗ podman selinux: container with label=disable
   (from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 866,
    from function `check_label' in file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 23,
    in test file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 44)
     `check_label "--security-opt label=disable" "spc_t"' failed
   [07:30:08.218197478] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:08.263169540] # podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
   [07:30:08.308358956] # podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   [07:30:08.369337766] quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
   [07:30:08.384923721] # podman run --rm --security-opt label=disable quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 cat -v /proc/self/attr/current
   [07:30:08.790903629] unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: SELinux role should always be system_r
   #| expected: '.*_u:system_r:.*' (using expr)
   #|   actual: 'unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   \# [teardown]
   [07:30:08.813507569] # podman pod rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:08.851230232] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:08.888425184] # podman network prune --force
   [07:30:08.925716285] # podman volume rm -a -f
 ✗ podman selinux: privileged container
   (from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 866,
    from function `check_label' in file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 23,
    in test file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 49)
     `check_label "--privileged --userns=host" "spc_t"' failed
   [07:30:09.006790689] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:09.052849978] # podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
   [07:30:09.099957334] # podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   [07:30:09.144952275] quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
   [07:30:09.161027355] # podman run --rm --privileged --userns=host quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 cat -v /proc/self/attr/current
   [07:30:09.577332235] unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: SELinux role should always be system_r
   #| expected: '.*_u:system_r:.*' (using expr)
   #|   actual: 'unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   \# [teardown]
   [07:30:09.597826044] # podman pod rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:09.638833311] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:09.677205159] # podman network prune --force
   [07:30:09.716751043] # podman volume rm -a -f
 ✓ podman selinux: init container
 ✓ podman selinux: init container with --security-opt type
 ✓ podman selinux: init container with --security-opt level&type
 ✓ podman selinux: init container with --security-opt level
 ✗ podman selinux: pid=host
   (from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 866,
    from function `check_label' in file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 23,
    in test file test/system/410-selinux.bats, line 81)
     `check_label "--pid=host" "spc_t"' failed
   [07:30:12.967485273] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:13.011452598] # podman ps --all --external --format {{.ID}} {{.Names}}
   [07:30:13.056846889] # podman images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
   [07:30:13.100169856] quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 f5a99120db64
   [07:30:13.117306938] # podman run --rm --pid=host quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 cat -v /proc/self/attr/current
   [07:30:13.533054092] unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: SELinux role should always be system_r
   #| expected: '.*_u:system_r:.*' (using expr)
   #|   actual: 'unconfined_u:unconfined_r:spc_t:s0^@'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   \# [teardown]
   [07:30:13.554592415] # podman pod rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:13.597741210] # podman rm -t 0 --all --force --ignore
   [07:30:13.634962794] # podman network prune --force
   [07:30:13.672686163] # podman volume rm -a -f
 ✓ podman selinux: container with overridden range
 ✓ podman selinux: inspect kvm labels
 ✓ podman selinux: inspect multiple labels
 ✓ podman selinux: shared context in (some) namespaces
 ✓ podman selinux: containers in pods share full context
 ✓ podman selinux: containers in --no-infra pods do not share context
 ✓ podman with nonexistent labels
 ✓ podman selinux: check relabel
 ✓ podman selinux nested

17 tests, 3 failures

### Describe the results you expected

fix them.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @edsantiago @rhatdan can you peek at it?  I asked @chuanchang to open an issue for that.  Note that the tests pass on a fresh machine and when being run entirely.
Okay, I'm seeing it on 9.3. It looks like that SELinux test got a major rewrite in #18612, but that is not in the 4.6 branch. @rhatdan can you shed light on this please?

> Note that the tests pass on a fresh machine and when being run entirely.

I need to correct my statement.  It fails even on a fresh host.
Anyhow, I guess the solution is to waive these test failures until v4.7
Yes the tests changed on newer versions of selinux-policy which will never be updated in RHEL9 or earlier.

Would backporting https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18612/ to the v4.6.1-rhel branch work and make it partially conditional on the RHEL host?

Or is it nothing to worry about?
I'm OK with backporting, assuming all the approvals/etc can be overcome
@rhatdan can we just backport or is there any host dependency we need to consider?
I am fine with backporting the section that hanldes the SELinux label test.
What is the status here? Can this be closed?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
